description: Fix all mobile view issues across the OpenTabs documentation site to achieve full compatibility with mobile devices. Addresses tabs scroll affordance, code block viewport consumption, homepage grid breakpoints, breadcrumb overflow, hero typography, footer stacking, step indicator alignment, and touch-target sizing.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Add scroll affordance indicators to RetroTabs on mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want a visual indicator that the tab list is horizontally scrollable so that I can discover off-screen tabs without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: When the tab list overflows horizontally, gradient fade masks appear on the left/right edges to indicate more content
The fade mask on the left only appears when scrolled away from the start
The fade mask on the right only appears when there is more content to scroll to
The fade masks do not interfere with tab click/tap targets
The hidden scrollbar CSS (.retro-tabs-list) is preserved
Tabs that fit without overflow show no fade masks
cd docs && bun run build passes (next build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/components/retro-tabs.tsx (line 74). The TabsList has classes 'flex flex-row flex-nowrap space-x-2 overflow-x-auto border-b-2 pb-0' with hidden scrollbar via CSS (.retro-tabs-list in docs/app/global.css lines 384-390). Solution: wrap the TabsList in a relative container. Use a useEffect + scroll event listener (or IntersectionObserver on sentinel elements at start/end) to track whether the list is scrolled to the start/end. Render CSS gradient masks using pseudo-elements or sibling divs with 'pointer-events-none absolute inset-y-0 w-6 z-10' and a gradient from background to transparent. The RetroUI source at ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Tab.tsx does NOT have scroll affordance (it uses the same overflow-x-auto pattern). This is an improvement over the upstream RetroUI. Use useState for canScrollLeft/canScrollRight booleans, and useRef for the scroll container. The component is already 'use client'.

userStories.title: Responsive code block max-height on mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want code blocks to not consume nearly my entire viewport so that I can still see surrounding page content while reading code.
acceptanceCriteria: Code blocks use max-h-[400px] on screens below sm (640px) instead of max-h-[600px]
Code blocks still use max-h-[600px] on sm+ screens
Both vertical and horizontal scrolling still work within the code block
The floating copy button is still visible and accessible on mobile
cd docs && bun run build passes (next build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/components/retroui-codeblock.tsx, line 132. Change 'max-h-[600px]' to 'max-h-[400px] sm:max-h-[600px]'. This is a one-line Tailwind class change. The viewport div's full className is: 'fd-scroll-container max-h-[600px] overflow-auto bg-(--shiki-light-bg) p-4 font-mono dark:bg-(--shiki-dark-bg)'. 400px is ~60% of a typical 667px mobile viewport height (iPhone SE), leaving room for the header, breadcrumb, and scroll context.

userStories.title: Add intermediate 2-column grid for How It Works section
userStories.description: As a tablet user viewing the homepage, I want the 'How it works' section to show 2 columns at the md breakpoint instead of jumping straight to 3, so the cards don't feel cramped at 768px.
acceptanceCriteria: Below sm (640px): 1 column layout
At sm-md (640-767px): 1 column layout (unchanged)
At md-lg (768-1023px): 2 columns instead of 3
At lg+ (1024px+): 3 columns
The visual design of each step card is unchanged
cd docs && bun run build passes (next build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/app/page.tsx, line 94. Change 'grid grid-cols-1 gap-6 md:grid-cols-3' to 'grid grid-cols-1 gap-6 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3'. This matches the pattern already used by the features grid on line 76 ('grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3'). The RetroUI source uses similar progressive column increases ('grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3' in marketing page).

userStories.title: Smooth hero heading typography scale for mobile and tablets
userStories.description: As a mobile/tablet user, I want the hero heading to scale smoothly across breakpoints so it doesn't feel oversized at exactly 768px.
acceptanceCriteria: The h1 uses text-3xl on base (below sm)
The h1 uses text-4xl at sm (640px) — intermediate step added
The h1 uses text-5xl at md (768px)
The h1 uses text-7xl at lg (1024px) — moved from md to lg
The highlighted 'any web app' span text is visually balanced at all sizes
cd docs && bun run build passes (next build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/app/page.tsx, line 51. Current: 'text-3xl leading-tight font-bold sm:text-5xl md:text-7xl'. Change to: 'text-3xl leading-tight font-bold sm:text-4xl md:text-5xl lg:text-7xl'. This creates a smoother progression: 1.875rem -> 2.25rem -> 3rem -> 4.5rem. The RetroUI source uses similar incremental scales: 'text-4xl lg:text-5xl' for h1, 'text-5xl lg:text-6xl' for hero headings.

userStories.title: Truncate breadcrumb link text on mobile to prevent multi-line wrapping
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want breadcrumb items to truncate gracefully so the breadcrumb doesn't expand to multiple lines with long page names.
acceptanceCriteria: Each breadcrumb Link element has overflow truncation via max-w and truncate classes
On mobile (below sm), breadcrumb links truncate at max-w-[120px]
On sm+ screens, the max-width constraint is relaxed or removed
The final (non-link) breadcrumb item retains its existing truncate behavior
Breadcrumbs still wrap to a second line if there are many items (flex-wrap preserved)
cd docs && bun run build passes (next build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/components/retro-page-breadcrumb.tsx, lines 25-32. The <Link> element at line 25 currently has classes 'hover:text-foreground font-medium transition-colors' (and conditionally 'text-foreground font-semibold' for the last item). Add 'max-w-[120px] truncate sm:max-w-none' to the Link className. The non-link <span> at line 34 already has 'truncate'. The RetroUI Breadcrumb uses 'flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1.5 sm:gap-2.5' which is similar.

userStories.title: Stack prev/next footer links on mobile at md instead of lg
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the previous/next navigation footer links to stack vertically earlier (below md instead of below lg) since two side-by-side cards are too narrow on phones.
acceptanceCriteria: Below md (768px): previous and next links stack full-width (single column)
At md+ (768px+): previous and next links sit side by side in 2 columns
When only one link exists (no previous or no next), it fills the full width at all breakpoints
The visual styling (shadow, border, hover effect) is preserved
cd docs && bun run build passes (next build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/components/retro-page-footer.tsx, lines 31-46. Currently each link has 'max-lg:col-span-full' (stacks below 1024px). Change to 'max-md:col-span-full' on both the previous link (line 35) and next link (line 46). This makes them stack below 768px instead of below 1024px. At 768px+ in 2-column mode, each card gets ~350px which is comfortable. The grid container at line 31 uses 'grid-cols-2' when both exist, which is fine — the col-span-full override handles the stacking.

userStories.title: Ensure touch targets meet 44px minimum on all interactive elements
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want all buttons and interactive elements to have a minimum 44x44px touch target so they are easy to tap accurately.
acceptanceCriteria: The theme toggle button in GlobalHeader has a min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] touch target
The search button in GlobalHeader has a min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] touch target
The sidebar trigger (hamburger) in GlobalHeader has a min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] touch target
The code block copy button has a min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] touch target on mobile
Accordion trigger items have min-h-[44px] tap targets on mobile
Tab trigger buttons have min-h-[44px] on mobile
Visual button size may remain compact — touch target expansion uses padding or min dimensions
cd docs && bun run build passes (next build)
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
Multiple files need touch target adjustments. Use the pattern of adding responsive min-height/min-width on mobile only:

1. docs/components/global-header.tsx: The theme toggle, search button, and sidebar trigger all use 'p-2' with 16px icons = ~36px total. Add 'min-h-11 min-w-11' (44px) to each button. These classes apply at all breakpoints but the buttons are already larger on desktop due to shadow/border, so the min dimensions only matter on mobile.

2. docs/components/retro-theme-toggle.tsx: Read this file to find the button element and add min-h-11 min-w-11.

3. docs/components/retroui-codeblock.tsx line 44: The copy button has 'p-1.5' with 14px icon = ~30px. Add 'min-h-11 min-w-11 sm:min-h-0 sm:min-w-0' to make it a 44px target on mobile but keep compact on desktop. Actually, just use 'min-h-11 min-w-11' — it won't look bad on desktop either since it's a small button.

4. docs/components/retro-tabs.tsx line 80-83: Tab triggers have 'px-4 py-1' which is ~28px tall. Add 'min-h-11' to ensure 44px height.

5. docs/components/retro-accordion.tsx: Read to check trigger height — if it's already >= 44px from 'px-4 py-2' + text, no change needed. py-2 (8px) + text (~20px) + py-2 = ~36px. Add 'min-h-11' to the trigger.

userStories.title: Add mobile-safe padding to inline code elements
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want inline code elements to not feel overly chunky in dense paragraphs while still being clearly distinguishable.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (below sm), inline code uses border (1px) instead of border-2
On sm+ screens, inline code retains border-2 as it is now
Inline code padding remains px-1.5 py-0.5 at all breakpoints
Inline code text-sm and break-words are preserved

cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes

userStories.notes: File: docs/mdx-components.tsx, line 152. Current classes: 'bg-muted border-border border-2 px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-sm font-semibold break-words'. Change 'border-2' to 'border sm:border-2'. This reduces the border from 2px to 1px on mobile, making inline code less chunky in paragraphs while preserving the retro aesthetic on larger screens. The border-border color class stays the same.

userStories.title: Ensure homepage description text is properly sized on mobile
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the hero description paragraph to be readable and well-proportioned so the text doesn't feel too large relative to the smaller heading.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero description uses text-base on mobile (below md) instead of text-lg
Hero description uses text-lg at md and text-xl at lg+ (progressive sizing)
The max-w-2xl constraint on the paragraph is preserved

userStories.notes: File: docs/app/page.tsx, line 56. Current: 'text-muted-foreground mb-8 max-w-2xl font-sans text-lg md:text-xl'. Change to: 'text-muted-foreground mb-8 max-w-2xl font-sans text-base md:text-lg lg:text-xl'. This creates a smoother text scale: 16px -> 18px -> 20px matching the heading scale from US-004.

userStories.title: Add safe-area-inset padding for notched devices (iPhone X+)
userStories.description: As a user on a notched/island device (iPhone X, 14 Pro, etc.), I want the page content to respect safe-area-insets so nothing is obscured by the notch, home indicator, or rounded corners.
acceptanceCriteria: The html element in app/layout.tsx includes the viewport meta tag with 'viewport-fit=cover'
The body element uses env(safe-area-inset-*) padding for bottom safe area on mobile
The sticky header respects left and right safe-area-insets via padding
Content in the main area is not clipped by rounded screen corners

cd docs && bun run lint passes
